findings.evidence: Lines 161-168: the proof comment says `ElementaryTwoQuotient G` unfolds to the quotient and line 164 uses `rw [show Nat.card (ElementaryTwoQuotient G) = ...toAddSubgroup.index from ...]`; Mathlib defines `ModN` as `G ⧸ LinearMap.range ...` (`mathlib/Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/FreeModule/ModN.lean:26`) while `AddSubgroup.index_eq_card` is an `AddSubgroup` quotient statement (`mathlib/Mathlib/GroupTheory/Index.lean:348`).
findings.fix: Factor an explicit named bridge lemma/equivalence for `Nat.card (ModN (Additive G) 2)` versus the index of `(LinearMap.range ...).toAddSubgroup`, then rewrite through that lemma instead of the typed `show` defeq.
findings.issue: `card_elementaryTwoQuotient_eq_index_square` proves the cardinality-index bridge via `show ... from (AddSubgroup.index_eq_card ...).symm`, relying on definitional equality across `ModN`, `Submodule.Quotient`, and `AddSubgroup.index` wrappers.

summary: One public proof still relies on an undocumented wrapper-level definitional equality between the `ModN`/`Submodule` quotient and `AddSubgroup.index`. That makes the theorem brittle under changes to either quotient implementation.
